input.prompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

input.initialPrompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Check dotnet availability and binlog file size

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check dotnet availability and binlog file size

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze a .NET build failure using a binary log file (build.binlog). Let me start by examining the binlog file and extracting useful information from it.

Binary log files (.binlog) are MSBuild structured log files. I can use the `dotnet build` command with `/flp` or use the `MSBuildStructuredLog` tool, but the simplest approach is to use `dotnet msbuild` to replay the binlog, or use the `MSBuildLog` viewer.

Let me first check what tools are available and then try to extract information from the binlog.

arguments.description: Check dotnet availability and binlog file size

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which dotnet 2>/dev/null && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; ls -lh build.binlog", "description": "Check dotnet availability and binlog file size", "initial_wait": 10}

toolArgs.description: Check dotnet availability and binlog file size

arguments.command: dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | head -5; dotnet tool install -g dotnet-msbuild-structured-log-viewer 2>/dev/null || true
dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | head -5; dotnet tool install -g dotnet-msbuild-structured-log-viewer 2>/dev/null || true

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | head -5; dotnet tool install -g dotnet-msbuild-structured-log-viewer 2>/dev/null || true", "description": "Check for structured log tools", "initial_wait": 15}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet msbuild build.binlog -flp:logfile=build.log -nologo 2>&1 | tail -80", "description": "Replay binlog to extract errors", "initial_wait": 30}

arguments.command:
# Get all errors and warnings, and check if Api failed too
dotnet msbuild build.binlog -flp:logfile=build-errors.log -flp:errorsonly -nologo 2>&1; echo "---FULL LOG---"; cat build-errors.log 2>/dev/null

# Use binlogtool to get more structured info
binlogtool errors build.binlog 2>&1 || true

# Get all errors and warnings, and check if Api failed too
dotnet msbuild build.binlog -flp:logfile=build-errors.log -flp:errorsonly -nologo 2>&1; echo "---FULL LOG---"; cat build-errors.log 2>/dev/null

# Use binlogtool to get more structured info
binlogtool errors build.binlog 2>&1 || true
